---
title: Navier–Stokes (3D)
---

> Auto-generated 2026-05-06 19:23 UTC &nbsp;·&nbsp; 88 plots

3D incompressible Navier–Stokes on a triply-periodic domain with viscosity ν as the primary control parameter. The 3D extension admits helical structures, vortex stretching, and faster chaos onset than 2D: chaos horizon T* ≈ 8–16 s vs T* > 64 s in 2D (at ν=0.001, N=16). Gradient norms grow (vortex stretching) rather than decaying as in 2D.

::: {.callout-note title='Boundary conditions'}

Triply-periodic cubic domain [0, 2π]³; incompressibility enforced via pressure projection at each time step. No walls or inflow/outflow boundaries.

:::

## Initial Conditions

Visualisation of each initial condition available for this problem. IC plots are generated without running any solver.

### Abc

Arnold–Beltrami–Childress flow — a 3D Beltrami field that is a steady Euler solution with non-zero helicity. Particle trajectories are chaotic for A≈B≈C≈1, making it a demanding test for gradient signal at long horizons. Shape (N,N,N,3).


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/ics/abc/ic.png){.lightbox}

### Tgv3D

3D Taylor–Green vortex u=sin(x)cos(y)cos(z), v=−cos(x)sin(y)cos(z), w=0; divergence-free IC that develops turbulent vortex structures and a peak dissipation rate around t≈9/ν. Shape (N,N,N,3).


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/ics/tgv3d/ic.png){.lightbox}

## Forward

Forward-pass benchmarks: agreement between solvers, field diagnostics, and long-run stability.

### Agreement

3D forward accuracy sweep across ν for the 3D TGV IC at N=16. Solvers: exponax, phiflow, xlb, ins_jl, warp_ns, openfoam.

3D velocity magnitude fields and kinetic energy spectra for each solver, sweeping viscosity ν. Initial condition: 3D Taylor-Green vortex (N=16). Reference: exponax + ins_jl fine-grid consensus. Production results (N=16, steps=50, ν∈{0.001,0.01,0.05}, 6 solvers including phiflow after CG fix): 5-solver reference (excluding phiflow): warp_ns/openfoam best (~0.014–0.016), exponax (~0.017–0.018), ins_jl (~0.025–0.034), xlb highest (~0.036–0.048). phiflow: error ~0.096–0.100 vs 5-solver reference (valid after CG fix but highest error — numerical dissipation from semi-Lagrangian + CG pressure solve). 6-solver trimmed mean inflated ~12% due to phiflow outlier. Errors are ν-independent (consistent across all 3 viscosity values). warp_ns/openfoam cluster together (IPCS projection); xlb error consistent with LBM mass leakage (F-NS3D-1). F-NS3D-9 RESOLVED: phiflow explicit CG pressure solver (tol=1e-5) prevents 3D NaN at all tested steps/nu. F-NS3D-11: phiflow valid but least accurate at steps=50 (~10% vs 5-solver consensus); semi-Lagrangian dissipation increases with rollout length.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `nu` ∈ {0.001, 0.01, 0.05}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 16,
    "dt": 0.01,
    "steps": 50,
    "lbm_N_base": 16
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "nu",
    "values": [
      0.001,
      0.01,
      0.05
    ]
  },
  "fine": {
    "solvers": [
      "exponax"
    ],
    "dt": 0.002,
    "steps": 250
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/forward/agreement/fields.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/forward/agreement/fields_raw.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/forward/agreement/errors.png){.lightbox}

### Baseline

Single time-step agreement across grid resolution N at fixed ν for the 3D TGV IC.

Relative error vs N at steps=1; validates single-step forward accuracy across 3D solvers. Results (ν=0.05, dt=0.01): all 5 solvers valid at all N. Error at N=32: phiflow 0.0027 (best), ins_jl 0.0033, xlb 0.0071, exponax 0.013. Error at N=16: phiflow 0.011, ins_jl 0.012, xlb 0.025, exponax 0.052. Spread (std of errors) at N=32: 0.0035. phiflow most accurate; exponax spectral resolution marginal at N≤16.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `N` ∈ {8, 16, 32}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 16,
    "nu": 0.05,
    "dt": 0.01,
    "steps": 1
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "N",
    "values": [
      8,
      16,
      32
    ]
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/forward/baseline/fields.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/forward/baseline/fields_raw.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/forward/baseline/convergence.png){.lightbox}

### Physical Laws (Vs N)

Physical laws sweeps: divergence RMS and kinetic energy vs grid resolution N, rollout length (steps), and viscosity ν.

Divergence RMS and kinetic energy vs N / steps / ν for each solver; validates incompressibility and energy cascade in 3D. vs_N (steps=20, ν=0.05): xlb divergence_rms GROWS with N (4.7e-3→1.2e-2→2.0e-2, LBM mass leakage F-NS3D-1); exponax/ins_jl converge ~O(1/N²); phiflow div_rms=1.7e-3 (N=8) → 7.8e-4 (N=16) → 1.9e-4 (N=32), converging (CG pressure fix). vs_steps (N=16, ν=0.05): xlb div_rms persistent ~0.010–0.013 (LBM mass leakage, steps-independent); phiflow div_rms grows: 2.0e-4 (steps=5) → 3.9e-4 (steps=10) → 7.8e-4 (steps=20) → 2.4e-3 (steps=50) — semi-Lagrangian accumulation. exponax div_rms minimal at all steps. All phiflow points valid after CG fix (F-NS3D-9 RESOLVED). vs_nu (N=16, steps=20): all solvers ν-independent divergence (xlb ~0.01, exponax ~3e-4, phiflow ~8e-4 to 9e-4). KE decay is physical for all solvers. LBM N-growing divergence extends F1.1b to 3D. phiflow semi-Lagrangian div_rms ~8e-4 (N=16): higher than spectral/FD but lower than LBM; grows with rollout length.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `N` ∈ {8, 16, 32}

```json
{
  "name": "vs_N",
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "nu": 0.05,
    "dt": 0.01,
    "steps": 20,
    "lbm_N_base": 16
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "N",
    "values": [
      8,
      16,
      32
    ]
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/forward/physical_laws/vs_N/physical_laws.png){.lightbox}

### Physical Laws (Vs Nu)

Physical laws sweeps: divergence RMS and kinetic energy vs grid resolution N, rollout length (steps), and viscosity ν.

Divergence RMS and kinetic energy vs N / steps / ν for each solver; validates incompressibility and energy cascade in 3D. vs_N (steps=20, ν=0.05): xlb divergence_rms GROWS with N (4.7e-3→1.2e-2→2.0e-2, LBM mass leakage F-NS3D-1); exponax/ins_jl converge ~O(1/N²); phiflow div_rms=1.7e-3 (N=8) → 7.8e-4 (N=16) → 1.9e-4 (N=32), converging (CG pressure fix). vs_steps (N=16, ν=0.05): xlb div_rms persistent ~0.010–0.013 (LBM mass leakage, steps-independent); phiflow div_rms grows: 2.0e-4 (steps=5) → 3.9e-4 (steps=10) → 7.8e-4 (steps=20) → 2.4e-3 (steps=50) — semi-Lagrangian accumulation. exponax div_rms minimal at all steps. All phiflow points valid after CG fix (F-NS3D-9 RESOLVED). vs_nu (N=16, steps=20): all solvers ν-independent divergence (xlb ~0.01, exponax ~3e-4, phiflow ~8e-4 to 9e-4). KE decay is physical for all solvers. LBM N-growing divergence extends F1.1b to 3D. phiflow semi-Lagrangian div_rms ~8e-4 (N=16): higher than spectral/FD but lower than LBM; grows with rollout length.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `nu` ∈ {0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1}

```json
{
  "name": "vs_nu",
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "dt": 0.01,
    "steps": 20,
    "N": 16,
    "lbm_N_base": 16
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "nu",
    "values": [
      0.001,
      0.01,
      0.05,
      0.1
    ]
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/forward/physical_laws/vs_nu/physical_laws.png){.lightbox}

### Physical Laws (Vs Steps)

Physical laws sweeps: divergence RMS and kinetic energy vs grid resolution N, rollout length (steps), and viscosity ν.

Divergence RMS and kinetic energy vs N / steps / ν for each solver; validates incompressibility and energy cascade in 3D. vs_N (steps=20, ν=0.05): xlb divergence_rms GROWS with N (4.7e-3→1.2e-2→2.0e-2, LBM mass leakage F-NS3D-1); exponax/ins_jl converge ~O(1/N²); phiflow div_rms=1.7e-3 (N=8) → 7.8e-4 (N=16) → 1.9e-4 (N=32), converging (CG pressure fix). vs_steps (N=16, ν=0.05): xlb div_rms persistent ~0.010–0.013 (LBM mass leakage, steps-independent); phiflow div_rms grows: 2.0e-4 (steps=5) → 3.9e-4 (steps=10) → 7.8e-4 (steps=20) → 2.4e-3 (steps=50) — semi-Lagrangian accumulation. exponax div_rms minimal at all steps. All phiflow points valid after CG fix (F-NS3D-9 RESOLVED). vs_nu (N=16, steps=20): all solvers ν-independent divergence (xlb ~0.01, exponax ~3e-4, phiflow ~8e-4 to 9e-4). KE decay is physical for all solvers. LBM N-growing divergence extends F1.1b to 3D. phiflow semi-Lagrangian div_rms ~8e-4 (N=16): higher than spectral/FD but lower than LBM; grows with rollout length.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `steps` ∈ {5, 10, 20, 50}

```json
{
  "name": "vs_steps",
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "nu": 0.05,
    "dt": 0.01,
    "N": 16,
    "lbm_N_base": 16
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "steps",
    "values": [
      5,
      10,
      20,
      50
    ]
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/forward/physical_laws/vs_steps/physical_laws.png){.lightbox}

## Gradient

Gradient benchmarks: finite-difference checks and gradient quality (magnitude, direction cosine) across parameter, resolution, and horizon sweeps.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

**Differentiability Table**

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 8,
    "nu": 0.01,
    "dt": 0.05,
    "steps": 5
  },
  "fd": {
    "eps": 0.001
  }
}
```

**Fd Check**

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 16,
    "nu": 0.001,
    "dt": 0.05,
    "steps": 10
  },
  "fd": {
    "eps_values": [
      5.0,
      1.0,
      0.1,
      0.01,
      0.001,
      0.0001
    ],
    "n_dirs": 10
  }
}
```

**Horizon Sweep**

Sweeps `steps` ∈ {10, 20, 40, 80, 160}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 16,
    "nu": 0.001,
    "dt": 0.05
  },
  "fd": {
    "eps_values": [
      1.0,
      0.1,
      0.01,
      0.001
    ],
    "n_dirs": 8
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "steps",
    "values": [
      10,
      20,
      40,
      80,
      160
    ]
  }
}
```

**Horizon Sweep Limits**

Sweeps `steps` ∈ {40, 80, 160, 320, 640, 1280, 2560, 5120, 10240}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 20,
    "nu": 0.001,
    "dt": 0.05
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "steps",
    "values": [
      40,
      80,
      160,
      320,
      640,
      1280,
      2560,
      5120,
      10240
    ]
  }
}
```

**Jacobian Svd**

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 8,
    "nu": 0.001,
    "dt": 0.05,
    "steps": 10
  },
  "jacobian": {
    "n_alphas": 41,
    "alpha_range": 0.3
  }
}
```

**Jacobian Svd Nu01**

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 8,
    "nu": 0.01,
    "dt": 0.05,
    "steps": 10
  },
  "jacobian": {
    "n_alphas": 41,
    "alpha_range": 0.3
  }
}
```

**Jacobian Svd Steps20**

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 8,
    "nu": 0.001,
    "dt": 0.05,
    "steps": 20
  },
  "jacobian": {
    "n_alphas": 41,
    "alpha_range": 0.3
  }
}
```

**Jacobian Svd Steps40**

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 8,
    "nu": 0.001,
    "dt": 0.05,
    "steps": 40
  },
  "jacobian": {
    "n_alphas": 41,
    "alpha_range": 0.3
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/jacobian_svd_comparison.png){.lightbox}

### Finite-Difference Check

FD gradient check for the 3D TGV IC at N=16. Reveals whether VJPs are correctly wired for 3D domains.

FD gradient error U-curves and direction cosine for the 3D Taylor-Green vortex IC (N=16, shape 16³). Results (N=16, ν=0.001, dt=0.05, steps=10, 5 solvers): xlb OUTSTANDING — cosine~1.0 at all ε values including ε=0.0001 (cosine=0.9982); ins_jl EXCELLENT — cosine~1.0 at ε≥0.001 (cosine=0.9970 at ε=0.0001); exponax GOOD — cosine=0.9995 at ε=0.01, degrades at small ε due to FP noise (cosine=0.404 at ε=0.0001); warp_ns FAIL — flat cosine~0.973 across ALL ε (systematic gradient direction error, no U-curve). phiflow EXCELLENT — rel_err~6e-5 at ε=5.0, ~3e-5 at ε=1.0, cosine=1.0000; root cause of prior flat-plateau bias (rel_err~2e-2) was semi_lagrangian self-advection VJP missing cross-terms from backtracking position; fixed by replacing with explicit Euler differential advection (vel + dt * advect.differential(vel, vel)) in the periodic step function. 3D finding: xlb VJP CORRECT in 3D (F-NS3D-2) — contrasts with broken 2D xlb VJP. warp_ns systematic scale error confirmed by flat cosine plateau (F-NS3D-3). phiflow gradient correct: rel_err < 1e-4, cosine=1.0000 at both ε values tested.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 16,
    "nu": 0.001,
    "dt": 0.05,
    "steps": 10
  },
  "fd": {
    "eps_values": [
      5.0,
      1.0,
      0.1,
      0.01,
      0.001,
      0.0001
    ],
    "n_dirs": 10
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/fd_check/fd_check.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/fd_check/gradient_fields.png){.lightbox}

### Horizon Sweep

Gradient quality vs rollout horizon for the 3D TGV IC at Re=6280 (N=16, ν=0.001, dt=0.05).

Gradient norm, FD error, and direction cosine vs rollout horizon (T = steps × dt) for the 3D TGV. Production results (5 solvers: exponax, xlb, ins_jl, warp_ns, phiflow): exponax: cosine(ε=0.01)≥0.999 at T≤4s (steps≤80); chaos onset at T=8s (steps=160, cosine(ε=1.0)=0.566). Gradient norm grows 68→240 (vortex stretching). xlb: OUTSTANDING — cosine~1.0 at ALL horizons including steps=160 (T=8s). Gradient norm 57-210. Best 3D gradient solver. ins_jl: EXCELLENT — cosine~1.0 at all steps. Gradient norm 51-58 (stable). Second-best gradient solver. warp_ns: CATASTROPHIC — cosine~0.975 at steps=10 (F-NS3.3 scale error); degrades to 0.5 at steps=40; 0.33 at steps=80 (grad_norm=34177, exploding); NaN at steps=160. phiflow: GOOD at short horizons — cosine~0.9998 at steps=10-40 (T≤2s); degrades at steps=80 (cosine~0.92 at ε=1.0 — chaos onset); FAILS at steps=160 (cosine=-0.33 at ε=1.0 — WRONG DIRECTION; semi-Lagrangian dissipation causes gradient reversal). Contrast: 2D TGV maintains cosine>0.96 at T=64s; 2D norm DECAYS monotonically. 3D TGV chaos onset (for exponax) at T≈8s, ≥16× faster than 2D. xlb and ins_jl maintain gradient quality through chaos onset — their VJPs are more numerically stable than spectral/semi-Lagrangian methods in the chaotic regime. phiflow gradient fails at chaos onset (steps≥80, T≥4s) due to semi-Lagrangian numerical dissipation amplifying in the chaotic regime.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `steps` ∈ {10, 20, 40, 80, 160}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 16,
    "nu": 0.001,
    "dt": 0.05
  },
  "fd": {
    "eps_values": [
      1.0,
      0.1,
      0.01,
      0.001
    ],
    "n_dirs": 8
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "steps",
    "values": [
      10,
      20,
      40,
      80,
      160
    ]
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/horizon_sweep/error_vs_steps.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/horizon_sweep/gradient_fields_exponax.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/horizon_sweep/gradient_fields_ins_jl.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/horizon_sweep/gradient_fields_phiflow.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/horizon_sweep/gradient_fields_pict.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/horizon_sweep/gradient_fields_warp_ns.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/horizon_sweep/gradient_fields_xlb.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/horizon_sweep/horizon_sweep.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/horizon_sweep/ucurves.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/horizon_sweep/best_eps_vs_steps.png){.lightbox}

### Differentiability Table

Differentiability table: FD check for v0 input w.r.t. output for all 3D solvers.

Heatmap of differentiability status for all (solver, input) pairs in 3D. Production results (N=8, ν=0.01, dt=0.05, steps=5, ε=1e-3, 5 solvers): exponax fully differentiable — v0 OK (rel_err=0.018), viscosity OK (1e-4), dt OK (1.5e-3), drag OK (0.001); ins_jl: v0 FAIL (rel_err=0.084 — N=8 resolution artifact; fd_check at N=16 gives cosine=0.9999), viscosity FAIL (rel_err=1.0 — zero VJP w.r.t. viscosity), dt OK (0.001); xlb: v0 FAIL (rel_err=0.165 — gradient wrong at N=8; xlb VJP passes fd_check at N=16), viscosity ERROR (500 from server), dt ERROR; warp_ns: v0 FAIL (rel_err=0.227), viscosity FAIL (rel_err=1.0), dt FAIL (rel_err=1.0). phiflow: v0 FAIL (rel_err=0.116 — semi-Lagrangian at N=8 coarse grid, similar artifact to xlb/ins_jl), viscosity OK (rel_err=5e-5 — excellent gradient), dt OK (rel_err=2e-5 — excellent). Summary: exponax fully differentiable (best); phiflow differentiable for viscosity/dt but not v0 at N=8; ins_jl missing viscosity gradient (N=8 coarseness masks v0 gradient); xlb v0 gradient correct at N=16 but wrong at N=8 coarse grid; warp_ns VJP broken for all inputs. v0 gradient failures at N=8 are resolution artifacts (coarse LBM/FD/semi-Lagrangian); N=16 fd_check confirms all solvers except warp_ns have correct v0 gradients.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 8,
    "nu": 0.01,
    "dt": 0.05,
    "steps": 5
  },
  "fd": {
    "eps": 0.001
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/differentiability_table/differentiability_table.png){.lightbox}

### Horizon Sweep Limits

Rollout-length limit sweep for 3D TGV at N=20: finds the VJP OOM/timeout/NaN failure boundary per solver. Each solver should run on its own dedicated GPU (pass --gpu-ids 0 1 2 3) so OOM precisely reflects a single 16 GB V100 budget. Per-step early stopping: once a solver fails at steps=S, all larger steps are skipped. Failure types recorded: OOM (GPU out-of-memory), timeout (>1200 s), container_died (Linux OOM-kill or crash), nan (non-finite VJP gradient), error. N=20 chosen so xlb D3Q27 state (~7 MB/step) hits 16 GB at ~steps=2300. fenics_ns_3d excluded (N=20 steps=40 ≈ 4000 s >> 1200 s timeout, measured). A warmup VJP at steps=40 is run before timing to exclude JIT compilation. XLB runs with XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_PREALLOCATE=false to avoid VRAM pre-allocation.

Per-solver rollout-limit table: step count at first failure, failure type, and wall time for each successful step. GPU solvers (xlb, phiflow, exponax, pict, warp_ns) expected to OOM or NaN; CPU solver ins_jl expected to show RAM growth with rollout length.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `steps` ∈ {40, 80, 160, 320, 640, 1280, 2560, 5120, 10240}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 20,
    "nu": 0.001,
    "dt": 0.05
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "steps",
    "values": [
      40,
      80,
      160,
      320,
      640,
      1280,
      2560,
      5120,
      10240
    ]
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/horizon_sweep_limits/horizon_sweep_limits.png){.lightbox}

### Jacobian Svd

Full Jacobian SVD and inter-solver gradient subspace analysis for the 3D TGV IC at N=8.

Per-solver singular value spectra and cross-solver cosine similarity for the 3D TGV IC (N=8, ν=0.001, steps=10). Solvers: exponax, xlb, ins_jl (warp_ns INFEASIBLE: 1536 × 18s = 7.7h). Cross-solver cosine similarity: exponax–xlb=0.754, exponax–ins_jl=0.704, xlb–ins_jl=0.578. Condition numbers: exponax=1.84 (well-conditioned), xlb=228 (moderate), ins_jl=2.3e12 (nearly singular). Effective rank: exponax 1516/1536 (full-rank Jacobian), xlb 1196, ins_jl 785. Gradient norms: exponax=1.36, xlb=1.08, ins_jl=1.19 (similar magnitudes). Key finding (F-NS3D-8): exponax Jacobian is most informative (highest effective rank, lowest condition); ins_jl Jacobian nearly singular (condition 2.3e12) suggesting redundant gradient directions; LBM (xlb) and FD (ins_jl) gradient subspaces disagree most (cosine=0.578) — scheme-level structural divergence.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 8,
    "nu": 0.001,
    "dt": 0.05,
    "steps": 10
  },
  "jacobian": {
    "n_alphas": 41,
    "alpha_range": 0.3
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/jacobian_svd/gradient_fields.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/jacobian_svd/jacobian_svd.png){.lightbox}

### Jacobian Svd Nu01

Full Jacobian SVD for the 3D TGV IC at N=8, more viscous regime nu=0.01.

Per-solver singular value spectra and cross-solver cosine similarity for the 3D TGV IC (N=8, nu=0.01, steps=10). 10x more viscous than the base jacobian_svd (nu=0.001); expected to reduce condition number and raise effective rank.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 8,
    "nu": 0.01,
    "dt": 0.05,
    "steps": 10
  },
  "jacobian": {
    "n_alphas": 41,
    "alpha_range": 0.3
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/jacobian_svd_nu01/gradient_fields.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/jacobian_svd_nu01/jacobian_svd.png){.lightbox}

### Jacobian Svd Steps20

Full Jacobian SVD for the 3D TGV IC at N=8, extended rollout steps=20 (T=1s).

Per-solver singular value spectra and cross-solver cosine similarity for the 3D TGV IC (N=8, nu=0.001, steps=20). Extended horizon vs base jacobian_svd (steps=10); probes gradient subspace alignment deeper into the chaotic regime.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 8,
    "nu": 0.001,
    "dt": 0.05,
    "steps": 20
  },
  "jacobian": {
    "n_alphas": 41,
    "alpha_range": 0.3
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/jacobian_svd_steps20/gradient_fields.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/jacobian_svd_steps20/jacobian_svd.png){.lightbox}

### Jacobian Svd Steps40

Full Jacobian SVD for the 3D TGV IC at N=8, extended rollout steps=40 (T=2s).

Per-solver singular value spectra and cross-solver cosine similarity for the 3D TGV IC (N=8, nu=0.001, steps=40). At T=2s the TGV is well into the chaotic regime; tests how gradient subspace structure evolves at chaos onset.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "tgv3d",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 8,
    "nu": 0.001,
    "dt": 0.05,
    "steps": 40
  },
  "jacobian": {
    "n_alphas": 41,
    "alpha_range": 0.3
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/jacobian_svd_steps40/gradient_fields.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/gradient/jacobian_svd_steps40/jacobian_svd.png){.lightbox}

## Optimization

Optimization benchmarks: gradient-based recovery of initial conditions or physical parameters, topology optimization, and drag minimization.

### Recovery Constant Ic

IC recovery from a zero initial guess (cold start). Optimizer starts from u=0 rather than a perturbed IC, testing gradient signal without any warm initialisation. Fixed rollout steps=100, rand_div_free IC, 3 seeds.

Final IC recovery error from zero-initialised optimisation (N=16, ν=0.01, dt=0.02, steps=100, rand_div_free seeds 0-2).


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `steps` ∈ {100}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "rand_div_free",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 16,
    "nu": 0.01,
    "dt": 0.02,
    "steps": 100
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "steps",
    "values": [
      100
    ]
  },
  "optim": {
    "ic_init_type": "zeros",
    "lr": 0.001,
    "max_iters": 500,
    "patience": 50,
    "failure_threshold": 2.0,
    "snap_interval": 20,
    "ic_seeds": [
      0,
      1,
      2
    ],
    "record_diagnostics": true
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic/recovery.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic/recovery_const_ic_diagnostics.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic/recovery_evolution_exponax.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic/recovery_evolution_ins_jl.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic/recovery_evolution_phiflow.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic/recovery_evolution_pict.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic/recovery_evolution_warp_ns.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic/recovery_evolution_xlb.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic/recovery_fields.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic/recovery_final_states.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic/recovery_sigma_1e+02.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic/convergence_curves.png){.lightbox}

### Recovery Constant Ic Bfgs

IC recovery with L-BFGS (cold start). Same setup as recovery_constant_ic but using L-BFGS with zoom line search instead of Adam.

Final IC recovery error from zero-initialised L-BFGS optimisation (N=16, ν=0.01, dt=0.02, steps=100, rand_div_free seeds 0-2).


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `steps` ∈ {100}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "rand_div_free",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 16,
    "nu": 0.01,
    "dt": 0.02,
    "steps": 100
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "steps",
    "values": [
      100
    ]
  },
  "optim": {
    "ic_init_type": "zeros",
    "max_iters": 100,
    "patience": 20,
    "failure_threshold": 2.0,
    "snap_interval": 5,
    "ic_seeds": [
      0,
      1,
      2
    ],
    "record_diagnostics": true
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs/recovery_evolution_exponax.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs/recovery_evolution_ins_jl.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs/recovery_evolution_phiflow.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs/recovery_evolution_pict.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs/recovery_evolution_warp_ns.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs/recovery_evolution_xlb.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs/recovery_fields.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs/recovery_final_states.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs/recovery_sigma_1e+02.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs/convergence_curves.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs/optimization.png){.lightbox}

### Recovery Constant Ic Bfgs Proj

IC recovery with L-BFGS + divergence-free gradient projection (cold start). Same as recovery_constant_ic_bfgs but gradients are projected onto the divergence-free subspace before each L-BFGS update.

Final IC recovery error from zero-initialised L-BFGS+projection optimisation (N=16, ν=0.01, dt=0.02, steps=100, rand_div_free seeds 0-2).


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `steps` ∈ {100}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "rand_div_free",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 16,
    "nu": 0.01,
    "dt": 0.02,
    "steps": 100
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "steps",
    "values": [
      100
    ]
  },
  "optim": {
    "ic_init_type": "zeros",
    "max_iters": 100,
    "patience": 20,
    "failure_threshold": 2.0,
    "snap_interval": 5,
    "ic_seeds": [
      0,
      1,
      2
    ],
    "record_diagnostics": true
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs_proj/recovery_evolution_exponax.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs_proj/recovery_evolution_ins_jl.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs_proj/recovery_evolution_phiflow.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs_proj/recovery_evolution_pict.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs_proj/recovery_evolution_warp_ns.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs_proj/recovery_evolution_xlb.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs_proj/recovery_fields.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs_proj/recovery_final_states.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs_proj/recovery_sigma_1e+02.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs_proj/convergence_curves.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_bfgs_proj/optimization.png){.lightbox}

### Recovery Constant Ic Gd


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `steps` ∈ {100}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "rand_div_free",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 16,
    "nu": 0.01,
    "dt": 0.02,
    "steps": 100
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "steps",
    "values": [
      100
    ]
  },
  "optim": {
    "ic_init_type": "zeros",
    "lr": 5.0,
    "max_iters": 1000,
    "patience": 100,
    "failure_threshold": 2.0,
    "snap_interval": 20,
    "ic_seeds": [
      0,
      1,
      2
    ],
    "record_diagnostics": true
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_gd/recovery_evolution_exponax.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_gd/recovery_evolution_phiflow.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_gd/recovery_evolution_xlb.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_gd/recovery_fields.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_gd/recovery_final_states.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_gd/recovery_sigma_1e+02.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_gd/convergence_curves.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_gd/optimization.png){.lightbox}

### Recovery Constant Ic Proj

IC recovery with Adam + divergence-free gradient projection (cold start). Same as recovery_constant_ic but gradients are projected onto the divergence-free subspace before each Adam update.

Final IC recovery error from zero-initialised Adam+projection optimisation (N=16, ν=0.01, dt=0.02, steps=100, rand_div_free seeds 0-2).


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `steps` ∈ {100}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "rand_div_free",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 16,
    "nu": 0.01,
    "dt": 0.02,
    "steps": 100
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "steps",
    "values": [
      100
    ]
  },
  "optim": {
    "ic_init_type": "zeros",
    "lr": 0.001,
    "max_iters": 500,
    "patience": 50,
    "failure_threshold": 2.0,
    "snap_interval": 20,
    "ic_seeds": [
      0,
      1,
      2
    ],
    "record_diagnostics": true
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_proj/recovery_evolution_exponax.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_proj/recovery_evolution_ins_jl.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_proj/recovery_evolution_phiflow.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_proj/recovery_evolution_pict.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_proj/recovery_evolution_warp_ns.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_proj/recovery_evolution_xlb.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_proj/recovery_fields.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_proj/recovery_final_states.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_proj/recovery_sigma_1e+02.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_proj/convergence_curves.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/optimization/recovery_constant_ic_proj/optimization.png){.lightbox}

## Cost

Wall-clock cost scaling with problem size N and number of integration steps.

Wall-clock and memory profiling vs grid size N and step count for all 3D solvers.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

**Spatial Cost**

Sweeps N ∈ {16, 32, 48, 64}, steps ∈ {10, 50, 100}

```json
{
  "physics": {
    "nu": 0.01,
    "dt": 0.01,
    "lbm_N_base": 16
  },
  "cost": {
    "N_values": [
      16,
      32,
      48,
      64
    ],
    "steps_values": [
      10,
      50,
      100
    ],
    "n_trials": 3
  }
}
```

**Temporal Cost**

Sweeps N ∈ {16, 32, 48, 64}, steps ∈ {10, 50, 100}

```json
{
  "physics": {
    "nu": 0.01,
    "dt": 0.01,
    "lbm_N_base": 16
  },
  "cost": {
    "N_values": [
      16,
      32,
      48,
      64
    ],
    "steps_values": [
      10,
      50,
      100
    ],
    "n_trials": 3
  }
}
```

**Vjp Cost**

Sweeps N ∈ {16, 32, 48, 64}, steps ∈ {10, 50, 100}

```json
{
  "physics": {
    "nu": 0.01,
    "dt": 0.01,
    "lbm_N_base": 16
  },
  "cost": {
    "N_values": [
      16,
      32,
      48,
      64
    ],
    "steps_values": [
      10,
      50,
      100
    ],
    "n_trials": 3
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/ns-3d-grid/cost/cost.png){.lightbox}
